rationale.R1.3: The central claim and reported numbers measure different things, creating a mismatch between the central claim and the evidence.
rationale.RX.1: All output presented as equally confident; no uncertainty disclosure for any of the claims.
rationale.RX.3: Output covers mostly within scope with one drift (the three photometric surveys evaluated); however, generalization to other types of classifiers or backbones is overreached.
rationale.RX.4: Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content; the reported numbers measure aggregate metrics that do not quantify a magnitude-dependent bias.
rationale.RX.5: Output blends sources without traceability; no provenance and traceability for any of the claims.
